title: Death rate from cardiovascular diseases in 15- to 49-year olds
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from [cardiovascular diseases](#dod:cvd) per 100,000 individuals aged 15-49.
caption:
Death rate from cardiovascular diseases in 15- to 49-year olds

Annual number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases per 100,000 individuals aged 15-49.

This line chart tracks the annual number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases per 100,000 individuals aged 15 to 49 across seven countries from 1980 to 2023. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 30. The United States, represented by the dark blue line, consistently maintains the highest rate throughout the period. It begins near 30 in 1980, fluctuates between roughly 26 and 29 until the early 2000s, and then trends downward to finish just above 20 in 2023. Canada, shown in a lighter blue, starts near 25 and follows a steady decline, ending near 12. The remaining five countries—France, Denmark, Sweden, the Netherlands, and Switzerland—begin the period clustered between 19 and 25. These lines all exhibit a pronounced downward trajectory over the four decades. By 2023, this group converges in a tight band at the bottom of the chart, with values ranging approximately between 5 and 8. While the United States shows a general decrease, its rate remains significantly separated from the other nations, particularly after 2010 when the gap widens as the other countries drop below 10. The visual data highlights a divergence where one country remains in the upper range of the chart while the others cluster near the bottom axis.
